_meta.description: Business entity structures — the 'what am I?' knowledge. entity_type keys align with docs/plans/research/tax-mapping-research.md §B (sole_prop | s_corp | c_corp | partnership | nonprofit). Consumed by onboarding (the not-sure diagnostic), the tax engine (W1.3-B forms key off these), Penny explanations, and marketing/compare pages.

rows.label: Sole proprietor / single-member LLC
rows.description: You and the business are the same for tax. Profit is yours; you pay income + self-employment tax on it. Most freelancers and one-person businesses start here.

diagnostic_questions.q: Did you file paperwork to become an S-corp or C-corp?

rows.officer_comp_rule: No W-2 for the owner. Money you take out is an owner draw (equity), not a deductible expense.

rows.label: Partnership / multi-member LLC
rows.description: Two or more owners share the business. The business files an informational return; profit flows to each partner's personal return via a K-1.

diagnostic_questions.q: Do you have two or more owners?
Did you elect S-corp or C-corp status?

rows.officer_comp_rule: Partners are never W-2 employees. Pay for their work is a guaranteed payment (Form 1065 line 10), never wages.

rows.label: S corporation
rows.description: A tax election (often over an LLC or corporation). Profit passes to owners' personal returns, but owner-employees must take a reasonable W-2 salary first — the #1 thing the IRS looks at.

diagnostic_questions.q: Did you file Form 2553 to be taxed as an S-corp?
Do you (an owner) also work in the business?

rows.officer_comp_rule: Owner-employees must take a reasonable W-2 salary (Form 1120-S line 7), kept separate from staff wages (line 8). Distributions above salary are equity, not wages.

rows.label: C corporation
rows.description: A separate taxpayer. The company pays its own income tax (21% federal), then owners are taxed again on dividends. Common for startups raising outside investment.

diagnostic_questions.q: Is the business a corporation that did NOT elect S-corp status?
Do you have outside investors or plan to raise venture funding?

rows.officer_comp_rule: Officers are W-2 employees (Form 1120 line 12 officer comp). Owner profit comes out as dividends (not a deduction) or salary.

rows.label: Nonprofit / tax-exempt
rows.description: A tax-exempt organization (typically 501(c)(3)). No owners; surplus is reinvested in the mission. Files an information return instead of paying income tax.

diagnostic_questions.q: Is the organization recognized as tax-exempt by the IRS?

rows.officer_comp_rule: No owners. Staff and officers are W-2 employees; compensation must be reasonable and disclosed on the Form 990.

forms_filed.note: Return of Organization Exempt From Income Tax
